name: llamafile
tagline: A whole chatbot in one portable file
description: Packages an LLM's weights and the llama.cpp engine into a single cross-platform executable using Cosmopolitan Libc. One downloaded file runs the same model on macOS, Linux, Windows, and the BSDs — no install, no runtime, no dependencies. A sibling, whisperfile, does the same for speech-to-text.
why: The most radical answer to AI distribution anyone has shipped: a model as a file you can archive, air-gap, or hand someone on a USB stick. Revived with fresh releases under Mozilla.ai after a quiet spell.
useCases: Distributing a model plus runtime as one artifact
audience: Developers, sysadmins, privacy-focused tinkerers, digital preservationists

setup.requirements: Download one file, chmod +x, run. Any modern CPU; GPU optional

license.notes: The tool is Apache-2.0; bundled model weights keep their own licenses.

limitations: Windows caps executables at 4GB, so larger models need external weights. Feature-wise it trails upstream llama.cpp and has fewer knobs than dedicated servers.
take: Fifty years from now, when someone wants to run 2020s AI, this is the format that will still boot.
